name: PlayerAuctions
description: A global two-sided marketplace where gamers buy and sell virtual goods, game accounts, currency, boosts and coaching, with escrow-style protection.

details.targetMarket: Individual gamers worldwide buying and selling in-game currency, accounts, items, power-leveling/boosting and coaching services

items: PlayerGuardian escrow that holds buyer payments until delivery is confirmed
After-sale protection / account insurance (7/14/30 day options) for account purchases
Formal dispute resolution and Final Resolution Committee
Game accounts, in-game currency, items/skins, power-leveling and boosting services, coaching
Custom offers and seller-created listings across hundreds of games
Free listing creation and seller levels (reputation-based)
Inventory management, delivery evidence requirements and disbursement options
Per-order service fees (buyer fee) and seller transaction fees (category-based, e.g., accounts),
Multiple disbursement methods (bank, Skrill, Coinbase/crypto in some regions),
Reserve / hold periods on payouts depending on product type, seller level and after-sale protection
Search and filters by game/product type, seller ratings and order history,

missingFeatures: No standardized subscription or recurring booking flow for coaching or recurring boost engagements (coaching is sold per session or offer-by-offer),
No public, documented API or official B2B integration for partners (site warns against unauthorized APIs),
No clearly prominent ‘verified professional’ seller program or structured KYC/skill badges for high-ticket sellers — trust still relies on ratings and PlayerGuardian evidence rules,
Opaque, layered fee structure (platform fee + disbursement/provider fees + optional after-sale protection) with limited fee-simplification or bundled pricing for sellers,
Limited seller analytics and conversion tooling (no public dashboard docs showing advanced funnel/marketing analytics to optimize listings)

pricingPlans.name: Register & Browse
pricingPlans.note: Account registration and creating listings is free; fees are charged only when an order completes.

pricingPlans.name: Per-transaction (sellers)
pricingPlans.note: Platform transaction fee applied on sale (varies by product category); disbursement/payment-provider fees are deducted separately.

pricingPlans.name: Buyer service fee
pricingPlans.note: Buyers pay a service fee at checkout to cover fraud protection and platform costs; amount varies by payment gateway and order.

pricingEvidence.label: Seller fees FAQ
pricingEvidence.claim: Registration and listing are free; PlayerAuctions charges a transaction fee (e.g., 12.99% + $0.99 for account sales) and disbursement fees are deducted based on chosen payout method.

pricingEvidence.label: Buyer Payments FAQ
pricingEvidence.claim: A buyer service fee is applied at checkout to cover buyer fraud protection and platform costs.

pricingEvidence.label: Reddit discussion on seller fee breakdown
pricingEvidence.claim: Sellers report a visible platform fee of ~12.99% + $0.99 and additional disbursement/provider fees, producing confusion about the final payout.

companyEvidence.label: About page
companyEvidence.claim: PlayerAuctions was founded in 1999 and describes itself as a global e-commerce platform for virtual gaming products.

companyEvidence.label: Crunchbase profile
companyEvidence.claim: Company profile listing PlayerAuctions as an online platform for buying/selling digital assets; public profile does not show major VC funding rounds.

companyEvidence.label: SimilarWeb company overview
companyEvidence.claim: Company data listing year founded (1999) and company size estimates used by market researchers.

reviewEvidence.label: Trustpilot reviews
reviewEvidence.claim: Thousands of user reviews with mixed sentiment: many positive notes about delivery/security but repeated complaints about disputes, banned accounts and fees.

reviewEvidence.label: Reddit threads
reviewEvidence.claim: Multiple community posts calling out high/complex seller fees and dissatisfaction with net payouts after disbursement charges.

reviewEvidence.label: Independent review / safety guide
reviewEvidence.claim: Third‑party guides highlight PlayerGuardian escrow as a strength but call out mixed user experiences and account‑ban risks.

featureEvidence.label: PlayerGuardian / Secure Buyer Delivery Agreement
featureEvidence.claim: Describes PlayerGuardian escrow protections, holding buyer payments until PlayerAuctions determines order completion and dispute procedures.

featureEvidence.label: After‑sale protection FAQ
featureEvidence.claim: Explains after-sale protection/insurance for account orders and how disbursements are withheld during protection windows.

featureEvidence.label: Homepage / product categories
featureEvidence.claim: Public marketplace listing for accounts, currency, items, power-leveling/boosting and other services across many game titles.

strengths: PlayerGuardian escrow and after-sale protection that meaningfully reduces buyer fraud risk compared with direct trades
Large, global marketplace with listings across hundreds of games and many product categories (accounts, currency, boosts, coaching)
Low friction to list: free listings and an established seller ecosystem (power-sellers) that create liquidity
Multiple payout/disbursement options and operational experience handling two-sided payments and disputes
weaknesses: Complex and opaque fee stacking (platform fee + disbursement/provider fees + optional protection) that frustrates sellers and compresses net payouts
Reserve/hold periods and disbursement rules can delay seller cashflow and create uncertainty for high-value sales
Marketplace attracts risky listings (allegations of hacked/modded accounts) and faces legal pressure from publishers (recent lawsuits), which increases compliance and reputational risk
Mixed user reviews highlight disputes over returns, long support interactions, and cases where after-sale protection was insufficient for banned/reclaimed accounts
Lack of clear developer/B2B API or partner program limits integration opportunities (no official public API documented)
